instruction: Classify the inspection note into a defect category.
baseline_prompt: You are a quality-assurance defect classifier. Classify the inspection note into a defect category. Classify into one of these categories: surface, dimensional, assembly, electrical, material, labeling.
source: Authored for TEI-Bench (Sonnet-generated, Sonnet-validated; kept only label-agreed examples).

train.query: Deep gouge found along the edge of the mounting plate, measuring roughly 15mm in length. Metal substrate is visible through the damaged paint layer. This unit cannot proceed to packaging.
Hole spacing between mounting points measures 45.8mm instead of required 50mm. Fails acceptance criteria.

Two of the four corner screws securing the main circuit board to the enclosure are missing. Board exhibits slight movement when pressure applied, which could lead to connection issues during transport.
LED indicator D7 does not illuminate when power is applied. Checked forward voltage across component and found no voltage present, suggesting open circuit in feed line from regulator.
Brittle fracture of weld joint at -20°C operational test. Base metal carbon content measures 0.45% versus specified low-carbon 0.15% maximum for cold service application.
The product arrived with a French-language sticker instead of the English one specified for the US market. Customer-facing information is in the wrong language for the intended distribution region.
Inspection reveals numerous fine abrasions across the display bezel. The protective film appears to have been removed prematurely, allowing contact damage during subsequent processing stages.
Wall thickness variance detected - measured 1.8mm in multiple locations where nominal is 2.5mm with -0.5mm tolerance.

During inspection found that the rear panel has been installed upside down. Connector ports are oriented incorrectly and the ventilation grille is now at the top instead of bottom as per specification.
Power supply module fails to deliver regulated 12VDC output. Measured output varies between 8.5V and 10.2V under no-load condition. Capacitor C15 shows signs of thermal stress.
Thread galling observed during assembly torque sequence. Metallurgy report indicates 304 stainless steel fasteners rather than called-out 316 grade.
Hazard warning symbol is missing from the chemical container. The safety data sheet requires a flammable materials pictogram, but the applied sticker only shows handling instructions.

The anodized finish on the aluminum extrusion shows uneven coloring with several light patches near the mounting holes. Buffing marks are visible under proper lighting conditions and fail aesthetic standards.
Overall length of bracket came in at 127.3mm, exceeds maximum allowable length of 125.5mm per print.
Unit arrived with the left mounting bracket completely absent from the chassis. All other components present and functional, but installation cannot proceed without this critical hardware piece.

test.query: Intermittent connection observed at terminal block TB2. Contact resistance fluctuates between 2 and 15 ohms during vibration test, indicating loose crimp or inadequate wire insertion.
The alloy composition analysis shows 6061-T6 aluminum was used instead of the specified 7075-T6, resulting in insufficient tensile strength for this aerospace component.
Barcode on the packaging is smudged and cannot be scanned by the reader. The digits underneath are partially illegible, making it impossible to verify the product code.
Significant discoloration and hazing noted on the transparent cover panel. The affected area spans approximately 40% of the total viewable region and appears milky rather than crystal clear.
Flange width measures 38.2mm but blueprint dimensions require 40.0mm +0.1/-0.0, part is undersize and out of spec.
Inspection revealed that the protective foam insert was never installed between the display panel and outer casing. This leaves the screen vulnerable to pressure damage during shipping.
Unit draws 3.2A at startup, exceeding the 2.5A maximum specified in design requirements. Observed voltage drop of 1.8V across main supply rail under load conditions.
Casting porosity exceeds acceptance criteria with multiple voids detected via X-ray inspection. Iron content in aluminum casting suggests contaminated feedstock.

Expiration date printed on the bottom of the container shows 02/2023 but manufacturing records indicate this batch was produced in March 2024. Clear date discrepancy that violates food safety regulations.

The powder coating on the left side bracket exhibits orange peel texture with uneven gloss distribution. Several areas show rough patches that do not meet the smoothness specification outlined in QC-405.
Measured shaft diameter at 12.47mm, spec calls for 12.50mm ±0.02mm. Outside tolerance range.
Found that the heat sink was mounted without thermal paste between it and the processor. This critical interface compound is completely absent, which will result in inadequate heat dissipation.
Short circuit detected between ground plane and signal line during in-circuit testing. Multimeter reading shows 0.3 ohms resistance where isolation should be greater than 10 megohms.
Chemical resistance test failure - gasket dissolved in hydraulic fluid within 48 hours. Cross-reference confirms nitrile rubber instead of Viton as per drawing.
The nutritional information panel has the allergen statement cut off at the edge where the sticker was misaligned during application. Contains wheat is not visible to consumers.
Observed a 2.3-inch linear mark running diagonally across the top panel. The mark appears to have penetrated the clear coat and exposed the underlying primer layer. Reject recommended pending rework.
Bore runout exceeds 0.05mm TIR specification, actual measurement shows 0.12mm total indicator reading.
The product shipped with the wrong model battery compartment cover attached. The current cover does not fit properly and leaves a 3mm gap, failing to secure the batteries in place.
